question.text: An ATM call option is trading at a premium of Rs. 180. What is the time value of this option?

answer.text: Rs. 180 - the entire premium is time value
Rs. 0 - ATM options have no time value
Rs. 90 - half the premium is time value for ATM options
Cannot be determined without knowing the spot price

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For an ATM option, Spot = Strike, so Intrinsic Value = max(S - X, 0) = max(0, 0) = 0. Time Value = Premium - Intrinsic Value = 180 - 0 = Rs. 180.
The ENTIRE premium of an ATM option is time value.
**The Trap:** ATM options actually have the HIGHEST time value among all strikes. They are not worthless just because IV = 0.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Similarly, OTM options also have premium = 100% time value (zero intrinsic value).
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ATM/OTM premium = 100% time value. ITM premium = intrinsic value + time value.
